questions.question: How do you change your display language in the Notion desktop or web app?

options.text: Go to Settings in your sidebar → Preferences → Language & Time
options.explanation: According to the article, you must navigate to Settings → Preferences → Language & Time and select your language from the dropdown.

options.text: Go to Dashboard → General Settings → Language
options.explanation: The correct path is Sidebar → Settings → Preferences, not Dashboard → General Settings.

options.text: Hover over the top left corner and click the gear icon
options.explanation: While you can access settings via the sidebar, the specific path to change the language requires opening Preferences and the Language dropdown.

options.text: Open the sidebar, click on the '...' menu, and select 'Change Language'
options.explanation: There is no 'Change Language' option in the sidebar '...' menu; it is located under Settings → Preferences.

questions.question: Which of the following languages is listed as an available language in Notion?

options.text: Russian
options.explanation: Russian is not listed in the provided table of available languages. The options include Arabic, Hebrew, and Vietnamese, but not Russian.

options.text: Korean
options.explanation: Korean is listed as one of the currently available languages.

options.text: Swiss German
options.explanation: While German is available, the specific regional variant Swiss German is not mentioned in the list.

options.text: Portuguese (Portugal)
options.explanation: The article lists Portuguese (Brazil), but does not mention Portuguese (Portugal).

questions.question: What behavior applies to the language setting on the mobile Notion app?

options.text: It matches the language setting of your desktop app exactly.
options.explanation: On mobile, the app language follows the language ranking in your system preferences, not the desktop settings.

options.text: It follows the language ranking in your system preferences.
options.explanation: The article explicitly states that on mobile, the app language follows the language ranking in your system preferences.

options.text: It requires a system restart to update.
options.explanation: There is no mention of a restart being required; the language updates automatically based on system settings.

options.text: It is locked to English regardless of system settings.
options.explanation: Notion allows mobile languages to change based on system preferences, so it is not locked to English.

questions.question: Does Notion automatically detect the text direction (RTL or LTR) in simple tables?

options.text: Yes, it detects the direction for all block types including tables.
options.explanation: The article specifies that auto-detection works for 'most text-based blocks, except for simple tables'.

options.text: No, simple tables do not support auto-detection.
options.explanation: The article explicitly states that Notion does not support auto-detection of language direction in simple tables.

options.text: Yes, but only if you are using a specific mobile device.
options.explanation: The limitation applies generally to simple tables in the app, not specific to devices.

options.text: Only if the table contains more than 5 rows.
options.explanation: The size or row count of the table does not determine whether auto-detection is applied.

questions.question: When you set your display language to Arabic or Hebrew, what happens to the interface?

options.text: The interface font changes to Arabic or Hebrew.
options.explanation: While the interface language changes, the specific 'mirroring' behavior is described as the interface being mirrored horizontally.

options.text: The interface is mirrored for those languages in both desktop and mobile apps.
options.explanation: Setting the language to Arabic or Hebrew triggers the interface mirroring in both desktop and mobile apps.

options.text: The interface text is replaced with English.
options.explanation: Setting the language to Arabic or Hebrew does not revert the interface to English; it applies the selected language and mirroring.

options.text: The interface becomes right-aligned automatically without any user action.
options.explanation: While the interface is mirrored, the article mentions that users can manually set text direction at the block level if needed.

questions.question: How can you manually set the text direction for a specific block?

options.text: Hover over the block and press Ctrl + Shift + D
options.explanation: There is no keyboard shortcut mentioned for this action. The method requires using the block menu.

options.text: Select ⋮⋮ in the block menu and choose Text direction
options.explanation: To manually change direction, you hover over a block, select the ⋮⋮ menu, and hover over 'Text direction'.

options.text: Double-click the block and select 'Direction' from the top toolbar
options.explanation: The direction controls are not found in the top toolbar; they are found within the block's context menu (⋮⋮).

options.text: Go to Settings > Preferences > Text Direction
options.explanation: Text direction controls are block-specific, not global app settings.

questions.question: Which toggle in the Preferences menu ensures text direction controls are always visible?

options.text: Auto-detect Language
options.explanation: The 'Auto-detect Language' toggle is not mentioned as the setting for showing controls.

options.text: Always show direction controls
options.explanation: To always show text direction controls (even for left-to-right languages), you must toggle on 'Always show direction controls' in the Language & Time preferences.

options.text: Mirror Interface
options.explanation: The 'Mirror Interface' is a result of setting the language to Arabic or Hebrew, not a toggle to show direction controls.

options.text: Block Direction Visibility
options.explanation: This specific toggle name is not used in the article. The correct name is 'Always show direction controls'.

questions.question: Which specific languages, when selected as the display language, trigger the interface mirroring feature?

options.text: Japanese and Chinese (Simplified)
options.explanation: While these languages are supported, they are not listed as the specific triggers for the interface mirroring behavior described in the text.

options.text: Arabic and Hebrew
options.explanation: The article explicitly states that setting the display language to Arabic or Hebrew triggers the interface mirroring in desktop and mobile apps.

options.text: Dutch and Danish
options.explanation: Nordic languages like Dutch and Danish are supported but do not trigger the RTL mirroring feature.

options.text: Spanish (Spain) and French
options.explanation: European languages like Spanish and French are supported but are not mentioned as triggers for the mirroring feature.
